utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon . i'm seeing miss russell . she's a 39-year-old female here for , what are you here for ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's my right upper arm . it hurts really , really bad .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so severe right upper arm pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i was playing volleyball yesterday , uh last night . um and i went to spike the ball , and the team we were playing , they're dirty . so um , somebody right across from me kinda kicked my legs from under me as i was going up , and i fell and landed on my arm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm , like right on your shoulder .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so this happened , what ? like 12 hours ago now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , seven o'clock last night , so a little more than that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you taken anything for the pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been taking ibuprofen every six hours i think , but it's really not helping at all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , what would you rate your pain ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and do you have any medical problems ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . do you take any medicine for it ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any surgeries in the past ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i had a lumbar fusion about six years ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . let's uh , let's look at your x-ray .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon . show me the last radiograph . so this is looking at your right arm , and what i see is a proximal humerus fracture . so you kinda think of your humerus as a snow cone , and you knocked the-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the top of the snow cone ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: the top off the snow cone . um , so i'll be gentle but i want to examine your arm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . are you able to straighten your arm ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i can just straighten the elbow as long as i do n't move up here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: as long as you do n't move your shoulder .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: go ahead and bend . okay . so your exam is generally normal , meaning that the rest of your body is normal

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but you've got some swelling and erythema-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . on that right shoulder . you've got uh , tenderness over your right shoulder . you've got normal pulses , and everything else is normal . any numbness or tingling in that right arm ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , so what we're going to have to do- the good thing about um , these kinds of fractures is generally , they will heal up without surgery . um , but we have to put you in a sling that weighs your arm down and pulls it down . so we're going to put you in a long arm cast and a sling , and then we're gon na check you in two weeks to see if those bones have realigned and if they have n't , then we're gon na have to talk about doing surgery at that time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm going to prescribe you some pain medicine . we'll do lortab 500- lortab 5 milligram .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , you can take one to two tablets every uh , six hours as needed for pain . i'll give you 20 of those .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and um , do you have any allergies to medicines ? i did n't ask .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i do n't have no allergies .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , hey dragon , go ahead and order any medications or procedures discussed . um , do you have any questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . why do n't you come with me , we'll get the tech to put the cast on .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and we'll get you checked out .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 39-year-old female presenting with right upper arm pain.
The patient fell and landed on her arm while playing volleyball.
The patient has been taking ibuprofen every 6 hours without relief.
The doctor asks the patient to rate her pain level.
The patient rates her pain as 9/10.
The doctor asks about the patient's medical history.
The patient has a history of gallstones.
The patient takes Pepcid for her gallstones.
The patient had a lumbar fusion surgery 6 years ago.
X-ray imaging shows a proximal humerus fracture.
Physical examination reveals swelling, erythema, and tenderness of the right shoulder.
Physical examination shows normal pulses, and the patient denies numbness or tingling in the arm.
The patient will be placed in a long arm cast and sling, with a follow-up appointment in 2 weeks for repeat imaging.
The doctor prescribes Lortab 5 mg, 20 tablets, to be taken every 6 hours as needed for pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has any medication allergies.
The patient denies any medication allergies.

case_description: severe R upper arm pain; hx gallstones and lumber fusion
